content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer array nums of length N and an integer value.


In one operation, you may add or subtract value from any element of the array any number of times (including zero).
Each element can be modified independently, and values may become negative.
Your task is to determine the maximum possible MEX of the array after performing these operations.
**The MEX (Minimum EXcluded number) of an array is the smallest non-negative integer that is not present in the array.**

Function DeclarationFunction NamefindMaxMexfindMaxMexfindMaxMex

Parameters
numsnumsnums : A reference to an integer array of size NNN.
valuevaluevalue : An integer that can be added to or subtracted from any array element any number of times.
Return Value
Returns an integer representing the **maximum possible MEX** that can be achieved after performing any number of operations.
Constraints
1ŌēżTŌēż101 \leq T \leq 101ŌēżTŌēż10
1ŌēżN,valueŌēż1051 \leq N, value \leq 10^51ŌēżN,valueŌēż105
ŌłÆ109Ōēżnums[i]Ōēż109-10^9 \leq nums[i] \leq 10^9ŌłÆ109Ōēżnums[i]Ōēż109

Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT ŌĆö the number of test cases.
For each test case:

The first line contains two integers NNN and valuevaluevalue ŌĆö the size of the array and the operation value.
The second line contains NNN integers ŌĆö the elements of the array numsnumsnums.




Output Format

For each test case, print a single integer ŌĆö the **maximum possible MEX** after performing the operations.

solution.approach:
Create a **frequency array** `freq` of size `value` to count how many numbers belong to each remainder class.

`freq[r] = number of elements with (num % value == r)`

Start from **mex = 0**, and for each next number:



Compute its remainder ŌåÆ `rem = mex % value`




If we have at least one number with that remainder (`freq[rem] > 0`):



Use it (decrement `freq[rem]`)
Increment `mex`



Otherwise ŌåÆ we canŌĆÖt form this number ŌåÆ stop



Return `mex`

Complexity AnalysisTypeExplanationComplexity**Time Complexity**One pass for counting + one pass for MEX finding**O(N + MEX)****Space Complexity**Frequency array of size `value`**O(value)**
